name: Mrs. Olaf Simonsen's Peanut Butter Pie
recipeInstructions:
The key to pleasing the palate of a Peanut Butter Pie connoisseur is to not mix the peanut butter into the filling. Note that everything is made from scratch.
This extra effort will reward you with excellent flavors. The following recipe is from the private collection of a deceased Swedish woman who, with her husband, Olaf Simonsen, operated a restaurant for over 25 years in Ft. Pierce, Fla.
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit.
2. Blend peanut butter and confectioner's sugar together until well mixed and mealy.
3. Spread about two-thirds of the mixture over the baked pie crust. Reserve the remainder for topping.
4. Combine flour, sugar, and salt in a separate bowl. Add scalded milk to the dry ingredients and blend until smooth.
5. Blend the liquid mixture into slightly beaten egg yolks. Return to double boiler and cook for about 2 more minutes.
6. Stir in butter and vanilla extract.
7. Pour the custard into the pie shell and top with meringue.
8. For meringue, beat egg whites until stiff enough to hold their shape. Add cream of tartar and beat until blended.
9. Mix sugar and cornstarch with the beaten egg whites and beat until very stiff.
10. Spread over the filled pie shell and sprinkle with remaining peanut butter crumbs.
11. Bake in a 350-degree oven for 15-20 minutes, away from drafts.
12. Cool completely before serving.
